summary: The primary marker that increases during hepatitis B infection is Hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg), because it appears early and remains elevated throughout both acute and chronic infections, reflecting continuous viral presence and replication. Unlike antibody markers such as anti-HBc IgM or core antigens—which indicate immune response or recovery—it specifically signals active disease progression through sustained detection.
